name: Joe Jordan
abstract: Joseph Taylor Jordan (February 11, 1882 – September 11, 1971) was an American pianist, composer, real estate investor, and music publisher. He wrote over 2000 songs and arranged for notable people such as Florenz Ziegfeld, Orson Welles, Louis Armstrong, Eddie Duchin, Benny Goodman, and others.

works.t: Oh Say Wouldn't It Be a Dream

works_created.t: Oh Say Wouldn't It Be a Dream

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: We had a little band called the oriental serenaders, Huntington High school. ... Joe Jordan was in there.

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Jordan was the delegate that, So Joe Jordan said, well if they don't open those doors down there, he say, you all come out and jump from the stage to the pit, see.

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Allen
about_quotes.evidence: while I was playing in this job, Joe Jordan come through, and one night, and they asked me how I'd like to go to New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: All them people was playing Scott Joplin's legitimate thing. Jelly Roll took it and slowed it down. ... Joe Jordan...

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: He was a big conductor, writer. So he took me to play one Broadway show called 'Fast and Furious.'

about_quotes.speaker: Henry "Jack" Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded with, uh, Joe Jordan's Tim Sharp. With whom? Joe Jordan's Tim Sharp.
